meta.title: Managed Agent Custom Tool Assumption Tracker
meta.purpose: Track every published managed-agent custom tool description that may contain stale runtime assumptions from the pre-Managed-Agents deployment model.
meta.source_of_truth: Published custom tool declarations imported into MANAGED_AGENT_TOOL_DECLARATIONS
notes: Descriptions are captured verbatim from each exported tool object in source, preserving string-concatenation and template-expression text where present.
Final statuses remain pending until Playwright + trace validation is executed in the live Sunder chat UI.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "USE THIS TOOL WHENEVER YOU HAVE A QUESTION FOR THE USER. Instead of asking questions in prose, " + "present options as clickable choices. Your questions will be presented to the user as a widget in chat."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes this still renders as a clickable chat widget and remains the preferred clarification mechanism instead of prose questions.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: You need clarification from me before proceeding. Use the proper clarification mechanism and show how the question is presented to the user.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Attach a file from the agent workspace to a CRM record. " + "This COPIES the file into the record attachments area, so the original workspace file remains unchanged. " + "Use this after generating a file the user should see in the record Files tab."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes copy semantics, workspace-path expectations, and record attachment UX still match the current product.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Generate a small file, then attach it to a CRM record. Before acting, explain whether the original file is mutated or copied and what file path you expect to attach from.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Browse public websites to search, filter, click, fill forms, and extract data. Provide a specific goal that states the site, actions, filters, fields to extract, and the desired output format."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes the browser tool still supports the stated navigation and extraction workflow in the Managed Agents runtime.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Use the website-browsing tool on a public site. Before acting, explain what inputs you need and what result shape you expect back.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Calculate traffic-aware driving time between two addresses using Google Maps Routes API."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes provider availability and the returned drive-time fields still match the described behavior.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Calculate the drive time between two Singapore addresses and explain what external service or result fields you rely on.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Update CRM vocabulary and custom field definitions for the current client. " + "Accepts partial updates and always returns the fully resolved resulting config. " + "If requested removals would affect existing records, it returns a warning instead. " + "Re-call with confirm_removals: true to proceed. " + "Data Modification Warning: Only use this after the user has explicitly asked to configure or reconfigure CRM. " + "Requires user approval before execution."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes approval, removal-warning flow, and partial-update semantics still behave as described.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Reconfigure the CRM pipeline slightly. Before acting, explain whether user approval is required and how removal warnings are handled.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description:
"Creates new Composio OAuth integrations.
Displays a UI card where the user can choose to create each connection or skip it.
Creating a connection authenticates the user to the service and then saves the connection to the user's account so they can use it in other agents in the future.

IMPORTANT: You MUST read /agent/skills/system/creating-connections/SKILL.md for detailed setup instructions before using this tool.

You can request multiple integrations at once.

For each connection creation request returns:
- userAction: 'created' if user authorized, 'skipped' if user declined.

If successfully created, also returns:
- connectionId: the new connection ID. Don't mention the connectionId to the user.
- tools: { activated: string[], deactivated: string[] } - list of all connection tool names by activation state
- connection-specific details"
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Contains a likely legacy path assumption (`/agent/skills/system/creating-connections/SKILL.md`) and also assumes the current UI-card and return-shape behavior.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Help me connect Gmail. Before acting, explain where you expect the connection instructions or skills file to live and what UI flow the user will see.
items.result_notes: Source still hard-codes /agent/skills/system/creating-connections/SKILL.md. That path assumption looks legacy relative to the current managed-agent skill mount story.

items.verbatim_tool_description: `Record a CRM interaction. Valid interaction types: ${CRM_DEFAULTS.interaction_types.join(", ")}. ` + "Data Modification Warning: Only record interactions when the user has explicitly asked to do so."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes valid interaction types and explicit-intent requirements still match the current CRM configuration and safety model.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Record a CRM interaction and first explain what interaction types you believe are valid right now.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Create one or more CRM records. Specify the entity type and field values. " + "Has built-in duplicate detection - if matching records exist, returns possible_duplicates instead of creating. " + "Set force_create: true to override. Supports batch creation (up to 50 records per call). " + "Data Modification Warning: Only create records when the user has explicitly asked."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes duplicate detection, force-create override, batch limits, and explicit-intent gating still behave as described.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Create a CRM contact, but first explain how duplicate detection works and what happens if a possible duplicate exists.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Create a new CRM follow-up task. " + "Data Modification Warning: Only create tasks when the user has explicitly asked to do so."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes task creation still requires explicit user intent and still maps cleanly onto current CRM follow-up behavior.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Create a follow-up task and explain whether explicit user intent is required before you call the tool.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description:
"PERMANENTLY DELETES a connection from the user's account. This destroys the stored credentials and cannot be undone. Requires user approval before execution.

WARNING: This is a destructive action. Only use when the user explicitly wants to DELETE the connection itself (e.g., "delete this connection", "remove from my account").
DO NOT use this tool if the user wants to remove or deactivate tools from a connection (e.g., "remove {connection name}") → use manage_activated_tools_for_connections instead"
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes destructive approval flow and the distinction between deleting a connection versus deactivating connection tools still holds exactly.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Delete a connection and explain what confirmation or approval behavior is required before the destructive step.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Delete one CRM record attachment. " + "This removes both the database row and the stored file. " + "DESTRUCTIVE: ask the user for confirmation before calling this tool."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes deletion removes both metadata and file contents and still requires confirmation before execution.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Delete a CRM attachment and explain what confirmation behavior you expect before proceeding.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Permanently delete one or more CRM records by ID. This is irreversible. " + "Supports batch deletion (up to 50 records per call). " + "For deal_contacts links, use link_records with action 'unlink' instead. " + "Requires user approval before execution."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes record-deletion irreversibility, batch limits, link-handling guidance, and approval behavior remain current.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Delete a CRM record and explain what approval behavior and irreversible consequences apply.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Executes a Composio action on behalf of the current user. Call list_composio_tools first to discover available actions for the app. Returns the action's raw output on success, or an error message on failure."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes Composio action discovery order and result-shape guarantees still match the current connection runtime.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Use a Composio action and explain whether you need to discover the action slug first or can call the action directly.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Get available tables, columns, and row counts for the agent SQL workspace."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes the SQL workspace metadata exposure and row-count behavior are still available exactly as described.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Inspect the SQL workspace schema and explain what scope of database metadata you expect back.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description:
"Gets detailed information for the listed connections.
Returns a full list of tools, including both activated and deactivated tools, for each connection, including full detailed descriptions and arguments if requested.
Also returns connectionId, serviceName, description, accountName, connectionType, toolCount, and other connection-specific details.

Use this to:
- Discover what actions you can perform with a connection before activating it
- Check which tools are already activated for a connection
- Verify exact tool names before activating connections"
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes the tool still returns the full connection and activation detail surface described here.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Inspect an existing connection and explain whether you expect activated and deactivated tools, argument metadata, and account details.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Read the current CRM configuration: valid deal stages, contact types, " + "company industries, interaction types, deal contact roles, and custom " + "field definitions for each entity. Use this before creating or updating " + "records when you need to know the valid values."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes this remains the authoritative read path for vocabularies and custom field definitions used by CRM tools.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Read the CRM configuration and list which vocabularies and field definitions you expect this tool to return.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Lists the capabilities available via the given integrations, including tools (if available), quality information (GREAT, GOOD, OK, LIMITED, and UNKNOWN), and notes."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes capability quality scores, tool lists, and notes still exist and are presented in the same way.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Look up an integration and explain whether you expect quality scores, tool lists, and notes in the response.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Create or remove a relationship between two CRM records. " + "Supported relationships: contact-deal (many-to-many via junction table, with role and primary flag), " + "contact-company (FK on contact), deal-company (FK on deal). " + "Data Modification Warning: Only link/unlink when the user has explicitly asked."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes supported relationship types, junction semantics, and explicit-intent gating remain correct.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Link a contact to a deal and explain which relationship types you believe this tool supports.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Returns the Composio actions available for a connected app (gmail, googledrive, googlecalendar, notion, etc.). Call this FIRST before execute_composio_tool so you know which action slug to use. You must have an active connection to the app (via create_connection) before this returns results."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes connection prerequisites and the discover-before-execute workflow still hold for connected apps.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: List the available tools for a connected integration and explain whether the app must already be connected first.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Lists all connections to external services that the user has already created in their account. Returns active, inactive, error, and pending connections with connectionId, serviceName, description, accountName, connectionType, and connection-specific details."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes connection statuses and returned metadata fields still match the current account-connection model.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: List my current connections and explain what statuses and metadata fields you expect back.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "List all file attachments on a CRM record. " + "Use this before discussing what files are already attached or before deleting one. " + "Returns storage_path and agent_path so attachments can be read via storage_read."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes attachment listings still include both `storage_path` and `agent_path` and that those paths remain valid for later reads.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: List attachments on a CRM record and explain whether you expect storage_path and agent_path in the response.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "List todos for this agent. Can optionally filter by specific todo IDs."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes todo state is still present and optionally filterable by todo IDs in the current runtime.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: List current todos and explain what filtering behavior you expect.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description:
"Activates or deactivates tools for connections. Requires user approval before execution.

Returns an array of objects for each connection:
- connectionId: the connection ID
- tools: { activated: string[], deactivated: string[] } - lists of tool names currently activated/deactivated for the connection
- skills: (optional) instructions to read the skills file for this connection.

Activated tools then appear in your tool context by their plain slug, for example GMAIL_SEND_EMAIL. If you don't see the tool you need, try activating it first.
To discover the full set of tools that are available for each connection before activating them call get_connection_details.

If your connection has an associated skills file you MUST read and follow the instructions in the skills file before using any tools from that connection."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes approval flow, tool activation visibility, and connection-specific skills-file behavior still work as described after the migration.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Activate tools for an existing connection and explain whether you expect any associated skills file instructions before using those activated tools.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description:
"Manage the agent's active triggers.

Actions:
- list: Returns all active triggers with their IDs, names, titles, invocationMessage, and arguments.
- view: Shows detailed information for a specific trigger. Requires trigger_instance_id.
- delete: Removes an active trigger. Requires trigger_instance_id. This is destructive.
- simulate: Fires a test event on a trigger to test the agent's response. Requires trigger_instance_id and payload.
- edit: Modifies an existing trigger's configuration. Requires trigger_instance_id. Use edit_params to modify trigger configuration (matching editSchema, not supported by all triggers) and/or invocation_message to set or clear the invocation title override.

Use list first to see available triggers and get their instance IDs."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes trigger-management actions, schemas, and destructive/simulation flows still behave as described.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: List my active triggers and explain what actions you believe this tool supports besides list.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description:
"Manage todo items for this agent. Supports batch operations for efficiency.

Operations:
- add: Create a new todo with a title and optional payload. todo_id MUST NOT be set.
- update: Modify an existing todo's title or payload. todo_id is REQUIRED.
- delete: Remove a todo to mark it as done. todo_id is REQUIRED.

You can perform multiple operations in a single call (e.g., add multiple todos, update several at once, or mix different operations).

Note: All current todos are visible in the agent's synced state."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes synced todo state and batch-operation behavior remain exposed in the current runtime.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Add and update todos in one operation and explain what synced-state assumptions you are making.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Create, list, update, or delete saved CRM views. " + "A view is a named filter+sort preset that appears as a pill tab on the CRM page. " + "Only create views when the user explicitly asks. " + "Filter keys match CRM columns: stage, status, type, industry, company_id, contact_id, deal_id. " + "For date ranges use column_after/column_before (e.g. due_date_before, close_date_after). " + "Use symbolic tokens for dynamic dates: $today, $week_start, $week_end, $month_start, $month_end."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes view UX, supported filter keys, and symbolic date-token behavior still match the CRM surface.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Create a saved CRM view and explain which filter keys and dynamic date tokens you think are supported.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Read the contents of a CRM attachment. Returns the file content for text-based files " + "(CSV, JSON, text, markdown) and always returns the original storage_path, derived agent_path, and browser download_url. " + `${DOWNLOAD_URL_EXPIRY_NOTE} Use search_crm with include: ['attachments'] to find attachment IDs first.`
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes attachment reads still return the documented metadata and still rely on the same attachment-discovery workflow.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Read a CRM attachment and explain what metadata you expect back besides the file contents.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description:
"Re-authorizes an existing connection that has expired or needs new permissions. Displays a UI card where the user can complete the auth flow to re-authorize the connection.

Use this tool if and only if there were authorization errors with a connection or the user explicitly asks you to.
The connection must already exist in the user's account.
Re-authorizing cannot change which account the connection is logged into in the external service."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes the reauth UI flow, usage constraints, and account-switching limitation still hold.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Reauthorize an expired connection and explain what constraints apply to account switching and when the tool should be used.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Renames the chat. Titles should be a concise 3-5 word summary that captures the goal and key tools. If the user requests a specific name, use that name."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes rename behavior and title constraints are still enforced the same way in chat.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Rename this chat and explain what title constraints you think apply.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Escape hatch for queries search_crm cannot express: multi-table JOINs, " + "aggregations (COUNT, SUM, AVG), GROUP BY, subqueries, date arithmetic. " + "Always try search_crm first. Read-only SELECT/CTE only. " + "Use get_agent_db_schema to inspect available tables and columns."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes the SQL workspace remains read-only and that `search_crm` still precedes most structured data reads.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Answer a CRM question that needs aggregation and explain why you are using SQL instead of search_crm.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Search current public 99.co Singapore listings using one or more 99.co search URLs."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes 99.co search inputs and current public-listing result behavior remain unchanged.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Search 99.co listings and explain what inputs you think the tool accepts.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Default tool for reading CRM data. Search any entity (contacts, companies, deals, interactions, tasks, deal_contacts, record_notes) " + "with free-text query and key-value filters. Returns matching records sorted by relevance. " + "Use `include` to fetch related entities in one call instead of multiple sequential calls. " + "Valid includes — deals: [contacts, interactions, notes, tasks, attachments]; contacts: [deals, interactions, notes, tasks, attachments]; companies: [contacts, deals, notes, attachments]. " + "Example: search_crm({ entity: 'deals', filters: { deal_id: '...' }, include: ['contacts', 'interactions', 'notes'] }). " + "For relationships without include: use entity 'deal_contacts' with a deal_id or contact_id filter. " + "For notes: use entity 'record_notes' with record_type and record_id filters. " + "Use this before creating records to check for duplicates. " + "For aggregations (COUNT, SUM, AVG) or complex filters, escalate to run_sql."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes entity coverage, `include` behavior, relationships, notes access, and escalation guidance still reflect the live CRM query surface.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Look up CRM data and explain whether you expect includes, relationship entities, and notes access in one tool.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description:
"Lists integrations that match one or more given keywords. Keywords are single words.
Searches integrations built by the Sunder team as well as integrations from Composio (over 3000 total).

NEVER mention integration quality scores or who built the integrations unless the user specifically asks.

Once you have the integration ID you can get more info using get_integration_capabilities."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes integration search coverage, hidden quality-score policy, and follow-up capability lookup still behave the same way.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Search for integrations and explain what hidden metadata you think should not be surfaced to the user by default.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Search Singapore property market data across CEA agents, CEA transactions, HDB resale transactions, and URA private sales. Use search mode for records and stats mode for aggregates. Large HDB and URA stats queries may return recent-window sample metadata alongside exact totalMatching counts."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes dataset coverage, search-vs-stats modes, and sample-metadata behavior still match the current market-data service.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Search Singapore property market data and explain what datasets and stats modes you expect this tool to cover.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Search past meeting recordings and their transcripts by keyword, date range, or linked CRM record. Returns title, summary, duration, and creation date. This searches recorded meetings, not calendar events."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes the search scope excludes calendar events and still returns the stated recording metadata.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Search meeting recordings and explain whether this includes calendar events or only recorded meetings.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Search current public PropertyGuru Singapore listings using queries or direct search result URLs."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes PropertyGuru search inputs and public-listing extraction behavior remain unchanged.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Search PropertyGuru listings and explain what inputs you think the tool accepts.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description:
"Search for available triggers by keywords.
Returns a list of trigger types that match the search criteria, along with their setup schemas and any prerequisites.

Use this tool to discover what triggers are available before setting one up.

The setupSchema field of each returned trigger describes the schema of the params object that should be passed into the setup_trigger tool.

Triggers that support editing will include an editSchema field describing the parameters for the edit action in manage_active_triggers."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes trigger-search result schemas and editability metadata still match the live automation system.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Search for available triggers and explain what setup information you expect back for each match.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Send a message to the user or another verified contact method. " + "Use 'owner' to target the account owner's primary email. " + "This environment currently logs the intent but does not deliver the message."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: This is an environment-behavior claim, not just a tool description. We should verify whether the tool still logs intent only or now delivers messages.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Prepare to send a message and explain whether this environment will actually deliver it or only log the intent.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Set up a new trigger instance. First use search_triggers to find available triggers and their setup schemas, then call this tool with the trigger ID and required parameters.\nOn completion, shows the user a UI card with the trigger details."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes trigger setup still depends on prior discovery and still ends with the same UI-card behavior.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Set up a trigger and explain what prerequisite discovery you think should happen before calling this tool.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Reads the contents of a file or directory by its path. If the path is a directory, returns a recursive tree-style listing of its contents. Image files and PDFs are displayed directly. Specify optional start_line/end_line for large text files. Use negative indices to count from the end (e.g., start_line: -10, end_line: -1 reads the last 10 lines)."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes durable-file path semantics, directory reads, and multimodal handling still behave exactly as described in the current runtime.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Read a durable file and explain which path shapes you think this tool should accept or reject.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Creates, edits, or deletes a file in the filesystem. Supports three operations: write (create or overwrite), edit (find and replace text), and delete."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes write/edit/delete operations and durable-path targeting still match the current storage tool contract.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Write a durable file and explain which operations you think this tool supports and what path namespace it applies to.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Update one or more CRM records by ID. Only provided fields are changed. " + "Pass null to clear a nullable field. Omit fields to leave them unchanged. " + "Custom fields are deep-merged (existing keys not in the patch are preserved). " + "Supports batch updates (up to 50 records per call) - all records must be the same entity type. " + "Data Modification Warning: Only update records when the user has explicitly asked."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes partial-update behavior, null clearing, custom-field merge semantics, batch limits, and explicit-intent gating remain correct.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Update a CRM record and explain how partial updates, null clearing, and custom-field merge behavior work.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Update an existing CRM task by id. Use this after finding the task via search_crm. " + "Only provided fields are updated. Omit fields you don't want to change. Pass null to clear a nullable field. " + "Data Modification Warning: Only update tasks when the user has explicitly asked to do so."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes task lookup-before-update, partial-update semantics, and explicit-intent requirements still match live behavior.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Update a task and explain whether you expect to find the task first before modifying it.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Reads a single webpage and extracts its content as markdown"
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes scrape output format and provider behavior still match the documented markdown extraction contract.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Scrape a public webpage and explain what output format you expect from the tool.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.

items.verbatim_tool_description: "Search the web for current information. Returns titles, URLs, and snippets."
items.concern_or_possible_assumption: Assumes the underlying web-search provider still returns the same result fields and current-information behavior.
items.tailored_validation_prompt: Search the web for current information and explain what fields you expect in the results.
items.result_notes: Validated in the live managed-agent trace and by source inspection of the tool implementation.
